---
title: "figure_3"
output: html_document
date: "2026-01-28"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggforce)
```

```{r}
# ── Input files ────────────────────────────────────────────────
esm2_file <- "/Volumes/T7 Shield/ESM2_F1_scores/winning_thresholds_esm2_l18.csv"
interplm_file <- "/Volumes/T7 Shield/layer_18_F1_scores/winning_thresholds_l18.csv"
```

```{r}
# ── Load ESM-2 (already winning thresholds) ─────────────────────
esm2_df <- read_csv(esm2_file, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  transmute(
    model = "ESM-2",
    F1 = winning_F1_score,
    threshold = winning_threshold
  ) %>%
  filter(F1 > 0.5)

# ── Load InterPLM winning thresholds ───────────────────────────
interplm_df <- read_csv(interplm_file, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  transmute(
    model = "InterPLM",
    F1 = winning_F1_score,
    threshold = winning_threshold
  ) %>%
  filter(F1 > 0.5)
```

```{r}
# ── Combine ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
plot_df <- bind_rows(esm2_df, interplm_df) %>%
  mutate(model = factor(model, levels = c("ESM-2", "InterPLM")))

# ── Compute means for overlay ───────────────────────────────────
means_df <- plot_df %>%
  group_by(model) %>%
  summarise(mean_F1 = mean(F1), .groups = "drop")
```

```{r}
# ── Violin plot ────────────────────────────────────────────────

library(tidyverse)
library(ggforce)

f1_plot <- plot_df %>%
ggplot(aes(x = model, y = F1, fill = model)) +
  # ESM-2 violin (keep narrower if desired)
  geom_violin(
    data = subset(plot_df, model == "ESM-2"),
    aes(fill = model),
    trim = FALSE,
    scale = "width",
    width = 0.4,      # <- control ESM-2 width here
    adjust = 2,
    alpha = 0.6,
    color = "gray30"
  ) +

  # InterPLM violin (can be wider)
  geom_violin(
    data = subset(plot_df, model == "InterPLM"),
    aes(fill = model),
    trim = FALSE,
    scale = "width",
    width = 0.9,      # <- control InterPLM width here
    adjust = 2,
    alpha = 0.6,
    color = "gray30"
  ) +


  # ESM-2 points (keep tight)
  geom_sina(
    data = subset(plot_df, model == "ESM-2"),
    aes(color = model),
    size = 0.4,
    alpha = 0.35,
    maxwidth = 0.25
  ) +

  # InterPLM points (spread wider)
  geom_sina(
    data = subset(plot_df, model == "InterPLM"),
    aes(color = model),
    size = 0.4,
    alpha = 0.35,
    maxwidth = 0.9
  ) +

  # Mean F1 as black point
  geom_point(
    data = means_df,
    aes(x = model, y = mean_F1),
    color = "black",
    size = 3
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "ESM-2"    = "#56B4E9",
      "InterPLM" = "#E69F00"
    ),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "ESM-2"    = "#2C7FB8",
      "InterPLM" = "#CC6F00"
    ),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.2, 0.2))) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0.5, 1.0)) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "F1 score"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 13)
  )

f1_plot
```

# calculating statistics between the distributions. 

```{r}
mean_ci <- function(x, conf = 0.95, nboot = 5000) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]  # drop NA / NaN / Inf

  boot_means <- replicate(
    nboot,
    mean(sample(x, replace = TRUE))
  )

  alpha <- (1 - conf) / 2

  tibble(
    mean = mean(x),
    ymin = quantile(boot_means, alpha, na.rm = TRUE),
    ymax = quantile(boot_means, 1 - alpha, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}
```

```{r}
# Mean + CI per model
stats_df <- plot_df %>%
  group_by(model) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean_ci(F1)$mean,
    ymin = mean_ci(F1)$ymin,
    ymax = mean_ci(F1)$ymax,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

stats_df
```

```{r}
plot_df <- plot_df %>%
  mutate(
    xpos = if_else(model == "ESM-2", 1, 2)
  )

stats_df <- stats_df %>%
  mutate(
    xpos = if_else(model == "ESM-2", 1, 2)
  )

# ── Violin plot ────────────────────────────────────────────────

library(tidyverse)
library(ggforce)

f1_plot <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = xpos, y = F1, fill = model)) +

  # ESM-2 violin
  geom_violin(
    data = subset(plot_df, model == "ESM-2"),
    aes(x = xpos, y = F1, fill = model, color = model),
    trim = FALSE,
    scale = "width",
    width = 0.6,
    adjust = 2,
    alpha = 0.6
  ) +

  # InterPLM violin
  geom_violin(
    data = subset(plot_df, model == "InterPLM"),
    aes(x = xpos, y = F1, fill = model, color = model),
    trim = FALSE,
    scale = "width",
    width = 0.9,
    adjust = 2,
    alpha = 0.6
  ) +

  # Mean ± CI (perfectly centered)
  geom_pointrange(
    data = stats_df,
    aes(x = xpos, y = mean, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
    color = "black",
    size = 0.4
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(values = c("ESM-2" = "#9c3d80", "InterPLM" = "#008080"), guide = "none") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("ESM-2" = "#6e1855", "InterPLM" = "#004c4c"), guide = "none") +
  
  # Correct x-axis ticks
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(1, 2),
    labels = c("ESM-2", "InterPLM"),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.15, 0.15))
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
  breaks = seq(0.5, 1.0, by = 0.1),
  limits = c(0.5, 1.0),
  expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0))
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = expression(F[1]~score)
  ) +
  theme_cowplot()

f1_plot
```

Doing the wilkox test
```{r}
wilcox_res <- wilcox.test(
  F1 ~ model,
  data = plot_df,
  exact = FALSE
)

wilcox_res
```
Interpretation: The distributions are different

Pannel B (counts)
```{r}
count_df <- plot_df %>%
  count(model, name = "n_features")

count_plot <- ggplot(count_df, aes(x = model, y = n_features, fill = model)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, color = "gray30", alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = n_features),
    vjust = -0.4,
    size = 4
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "ESM-2"    = "#6e1855",
      "InterPLM" = "#008080"
    ),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
  expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1)),
  breaks = seq(0, max(count_df$n_features), by = 500)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = expression("Number of features (" * F[1] * " > 0.5)")
  ) +
  theme_cowplot()

count_plot
```

# thresholds plot (panel c)

For means/labels:
```{r}
mean_ci <- function(x, conf = 0.95, nboot = 5000) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  boot_means <- replicate(nboot, mean(sample(x, replace = TRUE)))
  alpha <- (1 - conf) / 2
  tibble(
    mean = mean(x),
    ymin = quantile(boot_means, alpha, na.rm = TRUE),
    ymax = quantile(boot_means, 1 - alpha, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}
```

```{r}
threshold_stats_df <- plot_df %>%
  group_by(model) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean_ci(threshold)$mean,
    ymin = mean_ci(threshold)$ymin,
    ymax = mean_ci(threshold)$ymax,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

threshold_stats_df
```

```{r}
threshold_plot <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = model, y = threshold)) +

  geom_violin(
    trim = FALSE, scale = "width",
    width = 0.9, alpha = 0.4,
    aes(fill = model, color = model)   
  ) +
  geom_pointrange(
  data = threshold_stats_df,
  aes(x = model, y = mean, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
  color = "black",
  size = 0.4
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("ESM-2" = "#9c3d80", "InterPLM" = "#008080"), guide = "none") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("ESM-2" = "#6e1855", "InterPLM" = "#004c4c"), guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(
  breaks = c(0.0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8),
  limits = c(0.0, 0.85),
  expand = expansion(mult = c(0,0))
  ) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Winning threshold") +
  theme_cowplot()

threshold_plot
```
#===============================================================================
# Showcasing the strongest Interplm features
#===============================================================================
```{r}
top15_interplm <- read_csv(interplm_file, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  arrange(desc(winning_F1_score)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 15) %>%
  mutate(
    feature = factor(
      feature,
      levels = rev(feature)   # for horizontal plotting
    )
  )

top15_interplm
```

```{r}
top15_interplm <- top15_interplm %>%
  mutate(
    winning_threshold = factor(
      winning_threshold,
      levels = c(0, 0.15, 0.3, 0.4, 0.5, 0.6)
    )
  )

top15_plot <- ggplot(
  top15_interplm,
  aes(
    x = winning_F1_score,
    y = feature,
    fill = winning_threshold
  )
) +
  geom_col(color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(
  values = c(
    "0"    = "#f2fafa",
    "0.15" = "#b2d8d8",
    "0.3"  = "#519c9c",
    "0.4"  = "red",
    "0.5"  = "#006666",
    "0.6"  = "#003636"
  ),
  name = "Winning\nthreshold"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.5, 1.0)) +
  labs(
    x = expression(Winning~F[1]~score),
    y = "InterPLM feature ID"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = expression(Winning~F[1]~score),
    y = "InterPLM feature ID"
  ) +
  theme_cowplot()
top15_plot
```


# combining into final panel:
```{r}
library(cowplot)

# Top row: three plots
top_row <- plot_grid(
  f1_plot,
  threshold_plot,
  count_plot,
  ncol = 3,
  labels = c("a.", "b.", "c."),
  rel_widths = c(1, 1, 1)
)

# Bottom row: single plot spanning full width
bottom_row <- plot_grid(
  top15_plot,
  ncol = 1,
  labels = "d."
)

# Combine into final panel
final_plot <- plot_grid(
  top_row,
  bottom_row,
  ncol = 1,
  rel_heights = c(1, 1.5)  # adjust if bottom should be taller
)

final_plot

```






Saving the plot:
```{r}
ggsave(
  filename = "../../figures/paper_figures/Figure_3_F1_score_analysis.png",
  plot = final_plot,
  width = 8,
  height = 9,
  dpi = 400
)
```
